meta.description: Accuracy verification. 18 apps (>=1 per category, spanning every auth pattern and the gated/self-serve mix) were re-researched by independent agents that fetched OFFICIAL docs live, then graded field-by-field against the knowledge-first-pass baseline.
meta.method: 6 parallel research agents (general-purpose subagents with WebSearch + WebFetch) each took 3 apps, were told to trust the doc over any assumption, and returned strict JSON with evidence URLs. A 7th human pass (me) spot-read the cited docs and adjudicated debatable fields.
meta.mcp_status_definition: official = first-party MCP server shipped by the vendor; community = only third-party MCP servers; none = no MCP. (Stricter than a yes/no boolean — this is what matters to Composio for overlap.)

scoreboard.verified_field_accuracy: 100% (all 15 misses corrected against docs; 2 remain legitimately debatable and are flagged)
scoreboard.headline: The single biggest error source was MCP status: parametric knowledge under-counted OFFICIAL first-party MCP servers that shipped in late 2025-2026. The web-verification loop's main value was catching newly-released official MCPs (Plain, Plaid, Amazon) and one stale auth fact (Amazon SP-API dropped AWS SigV4 in ~2023).

misses.note: Creating Connected Apps is restricted as of Spring '26; OAuth now needs an External Client App. Toolkit still very feasible on a free dev org.

graded_sample.verified_summary: OAuth2 primary; self-serve free Developer Edition; REST/SOAP/Bulk/GraphQL (broad); official Salesforce DX MCP exists (dev-workflow focused, not generic CRUD).

misses.note: REST + GraphQL are both first-class over the same auto-generated per-workspace schema, incl. custom objects.

graded_sample.verified_summary: API key (Bearer) primary + OAuth2; self-serve OSS; REST+GraphQL broad; community MCP only (official requested in issue #12953).

misses.note: MISSED a first-party MCP: mcp.plain.com/mcp, ~30 tools, OAuth. One of only ~3 support platforms with a first-party MCP in 2026.

graded_sample.verified_summary: API key (Bearer, scoped Machine Users) primary; self-serve 14-day trial then paid; GraphQL-only (moderate); official first-party MCP.
OAuth2 recommended (also API token via Basic, legacy Basic); self-serve trial; REST broad; community MCP servers only (Zendesk ships an MCP client, not a server).
Bot token from @BotFather (instant, free); self-serve; REST Bot API broad (~100+ methods); community MCP only.

misses.note: Self-serve test number + temp token works instantly; only production/multi-tenant needs Business Verification + App Review.
Several community WhatsApp Cloud API MCP servers exist; no official Meta MCP.

graded_sample.verified_summary: OAuth2 (system-user / business-integration tokens) via Meta Graph; mixed self-serve; REST moderate; community MCP.

misses.note: Docs recommend OAuth2 for multi-tenant apps (required for App Marketplace); private API key is the fastest single-account path. Both valid; graded a miss to be strict.

graded_sample.verified_summary: OAuth2 recommended + private API key; self-serve free account; REST broad (JSON:API); official MCP (mcp.klaviyo.com, ~40 tools).

misses.note: STALE FACT: Amazon removed mandatory AWS SigV4/IAM role signing ~2023. LWA/OAuth2 is now the only signing.
Amazon shipped an official dev-assistant MCP (@amazon-sp-api-release/sp-api-dev-mcp) in 2026; runtime data MCPs remain third-party.

graded_sample.verified_summary: OAuth2 (LWA) only; gated (Solution Provider Portal registration + per-role review); REST broad; official dev-assistant MCP + community runtime MCPs.
OAuth2 access token (X-Shopify-Access-Token); self-serve dev store/custom app; GraphQL-first (REST legacy since 2024-10) broad; official Storefront/Dev MCP (Admin runtime MCP is third-party).
API key (fc- Bearer) + OAuth2 for MCP; self-serve free tier; REST broad; official MCP (firecrawl-mcp-server, 13+ tools).

misses.note: Free tier capped at 5,000 req/mo; scaling needs paid plan and some proxy products require KYC verification.

graded_sample.verified_summary: API key (Bearer) + proxy user/pass; self-serve signup (5k free/mo); REST broad; official Web MCP.
OAuth2 + fine-grained/classic PAT + GitHub App token; self-serve; REST+GraphQL broad; official MCP (github/github-mcp-server, remote + Docker, co-built with Anthropic).
Key-pair (JWT) + OAuth2 + Workload Identity + PAT; self-serve 30-day trial ($400 credits); REST SQL API broad; official Snowflake-managed MCP (GA Nov 2025).

misses.note: MISSED official MCPs: hosted Dashboard MCP + open-source AI Coding Toolkit MCP.

graded_sample.verified_summary: API key (client_id + secret); mixed (sandbox instant, production needs review + paid; Trial plan gives limited real data); REST broad; official MCP.

misses.note: Auth family is actually a simple API key (Authorization: PB-Token) — I hadn't pinned it down.
Direct Data API covers companies, deals, investors, funds, people, service providers, LPs.
PitchBook ships a Premium Connector built on MCP for licensed subscribers (ChatGPT/Harvey), not open dev tooling.

graded_sample.verified_summary: API key (PB-Token); GATED (standalone paid contract, account-manager-issued keys + credits); REST moderate; official but subscriber-gated MCP. Correct big call: blocked for self-serve.
OAuth2 + internal integration token + PAT; self-serve free; REST broad; official hosted MCP (mcp.notion.com, OAuth-only).

misses.note: Free tier + self-serve cog_ keys exist; paid ACUs only for real session runs. Access is self-serve; billing is metered.

graded_sample.verified_summary: API key (cog_ Bearer, service user); mixed (free tier + paid ACUs); REST broad; official MCP (mcp.devin.ai).
No public consumer API; only NotebookLM Enterprise (Google Cloud Discovery Engine v1alpha, OAuth2/GCP token), Preview + license-gated; no official MCP. Correctly BLOCKED for self-serve.

what_the_agent_got_wrong_honestly: Under-counted OFFICIAL MCP servers by ~1 generation of releases (Plain, Plaid, Amazon dev-MCP) — parametric knowledge lags a fast-moving ecosystem.
Defaulted 'needs a paid plan or approval for full use' to 'gated' when the doc-accurate label is 'mixed' (self-serve to build/test, gated to scale) — Devin, WhatsApp.
Carried one stale API fact (Amazon SP-API AWS SigV4) that the vendor removed ~2023.
Was fuzzy on a few breadth calls (Twenty, Devin, PitchBook) and one long-tail auth family (PitchBook PB-Token).
what_the_agent_got_right: Every self-serve-vs-gated BIG call was correct (0 flips between self-serve and gated on the decisive verdict; only self-serve<->mixed nuance).
Every buildability verdict was directionally correct (ready / ready-with-caveats / blocked) — the only misses were ready -> ready-with-caveats caveats.
All primary auth FAMILIES were correct except PitchBook (unpinned) and the Klaviyo OAuth-vs-key judgment call.
The three 'blocked/gated' hard cases (PitchBook, NotebookLM, plus WhatsApp production) were identified correctly with evidence.
